note_on_direction: Waypoints are ordered south to north (Kvikkjokk to Abisko), matching the actual hike direction. Ascent_m and descent_m are taken directly from the source table and already reflect this south to north direction, as confirmed by the author. Boat-crossing distances are noted per segment but excluded from km_from_start, matching the original trail-km markers, which stay the same across a lake crossing.

segments.notes: Latrine at Njahkájåhkå, around km 6 from Kvikkjokk.
segments.camping_good: Forest for most of the stretch, camping is fine almost anywhere

shelters.name: Stopover cabin Jågge
shelters.position: somewhere along this 23 km stretch, exact km not confirmed
shelters.notes: Unstaffed basic shelter, no shop. Some itineraries split this stage into two days using Jågge as the overnight point.

segments.notes: Motorboat + 3 rowing boats. WARNING: currents and shallows! 9:00/17:00 from the north, 9:15/17:15 from the south. 200 SEK. Contact Anne-Sophie: +46 70 673 55 (call the evening before a morning crossing, or at midday before an afternoon crossing). https://aktsebo.se/
11.5 km if skipping Skierfe and going direct; STF Aktse mountain cabin

STF Sitojaure Vaggevárasj, 200-350 SEK. Motorboat 7:00/17:45 from the north, 7:15/18:00 from the south. Contact the Blind family: 073-079 96 03, 070-277 66 88. http://www.sitojaure.com/#om-oss Note: the crossing actually spans two connected lakes, Gasskajaure and Gabddajavrre, per the official stage description.
segments.camping_good: Forest around Aktse, low above the valley
Forest sections after the crossing, sheltered bays

shelters.name: Stopover cabin Autsutjvagge
shelters.position: somewhere along this 19.3 km stretch, exact km not confirmed

segments.notes: M/S Langas boat crossing of Lake Langas (Sami name Áhkajávrre), operated directly by STF, about 15 minutes. See the kebnats_saltoluokta crossing for the timetable, price, and booking.

Primary: bus (operated by Road to Ritsem, not the old Bus 93 mentioned in the original source table -- that line no longer serves this route) or hitchhiking/taxi from Kebnats pier to STF Vakkotavare Fjällstuga, roughly 1 hour by bus. See the vakkotavare_kebnats_bus crossing for the current 2026 timetable -- note that timetable currently only has confirmed times for the opposite (Vakkotavare -> Kebnats) direction; the Kebnats -> Vakkotavare departure time needed for this leg is unconfirmed, and there is a known scheduling gap in the confirmed direction around 6 September 2026 that likely (but not confirmedly) also affects this direction. Fallback if no bus and no hitch: walk approx. 30 km along the road, +182 m / -109 m ascent/descent (figure supplied by the route author, not yet cross-checked against a source -- re-verify before relying on it).
segments.camping_good: All the way down before Vakkotavare

segments.notes: Motorboat (6-10 people) until 14 Sep. Twice daily: 8:00/16:00, 150-200 SEK per person
segments.camping_good: Down by the lake among birches (not on the open shore)

segments.notes: Side trip east: 5 km to a viewpoint; 14.3 km to Kebnekaise fjällstation (climbing); 18.5 km from Kebnekaise to Nikkaluokta (exit point)

shelters.name: Stopover cabin Kuoperjåkka
shelters.position: somewhere along this 12 km stretch, exact km not confirmed

segments.notes: STF Tjäktja mountain cabin along the way; stopover cabin at Tjäktja Pass
segments.camping_good: Down toward Alesjaure past the pass, or low ground before the pass

shelters.name: STF Tjäktja mountain cabin
shelters.position: 12 km from Sälka / 13 km before Alesjaure
shelters.notes: Smallest hut on Kungsleden, no shop, no sauna. Often skipped by hikers doing Sälka-Alesjaure in one long day. The actual Tjäktja Pass (highest point of the whole trail, ~1150m) is a few km further north of the hut, with a wind shelter at the summit.

segments.notes: Latrine at Rádunjárga, off the main trail (km 155.4-176.4 range).
segments.camping_risky: Around the big lake, no shelter

shelters.name: Rádunjárga stopover cabin
shelters.position: off the main trail, somewhere along this 21 km stretch (km 155.4-176.4 range), exact km not confirmed
shelters.notes: Unstaffed basic shelter, no shop. Has a latrine.

segments.notes: Windshelter and latrine at Nisson tältplats, around km 187.
segments.camping_good: Forest section below the tree line

schema_note_phone: phone (string, optional, absent when not known) is a direct contact number for a staffed waypoint, shown in its detail view only -- not surfaced as an itinerary badge. Currently only recorded for the three Fjällstation-class waypoints (Kvikkjokk, Saltoluokta, Abisko); not yet gathered for the fjällstuga-class huts.
schema_note_latrine: latrine (boolean, optional, defaults to false when absent) marks a segment that has a standalone latrine along the hiking trail itself, separate from the huts at either end (every staffed hut/fjällstuga/fjällstation already has one, so those aren't flagged here). Currently true for only 3 segments: Kvikkjokk->Pårte (Njahkájåhkå, ~km 6), Alesjaure->Abiskojaure (Rádunjárga, off the main trail), and Abiskojaure->Abisko (Nisson tältplats, ~km 187).
schema_note_shelters: The shelters array on a segment lists unstaffed emergency shelters or small huts passed along that segment. Where the exact sub-distance is confirmed from independent sources (currently only Tjaktja), position gives real km figures. Otherwise position is descriptive only, and should not be treated as a precise km value. The segment own distance_km, ascent_m and descent_m fields are unaffected and still describe the whole segment end to end.
schema_note_shop: shop.size on a waypoint reflects the original shop/transport column: full (well-stocked shop, matches Full+electricity marking), large, small, or none. shop.electricity is true only where the source table marked the lightning-bolt symbol (mains power available). Small unstaffed emergency shelters (listed under a segment's shelters array) have no shop by definition and are not included here.
schema_note_alt_names: alt_names lists spelling variants that may appear on physical trail signage, maps, or other apps: an ASCII fold (no Swedish a-ring/a-umlaut/o-umlaut or Sami a-acute) plus any specific alternate names found during research. This list is best-effort from remote research, not confirmed on the ground. Treat it as a search aid for matching a signpost to the right waypoint, not as a verified official naming registry.
schema_note_lake_names: lake on a boat segment is the name most likely to appear on the actual boat service, timetable, or ticket (what you would ask for or search when booking). lake_alt_names lists other names for the same body of water, such as the Sami name used in the original source table, which may still appear on maps or signposts. Confirmed by cross-checking against the Swedish Tourist Association boat-service page during research.
schema_note_alerts: critical_alert_id, where present on a waypoint or segment, references an entry in alerts.json by id. The app should look up that entry and display it prominently rather than treating it as ordinary reference text.
schema_note_ids: Every waypoint has a stable `id` (slug derived from its name at authoring time, never changes even if `name` is edited later). Every segment has a stable `id` (`<from_id>__<to_id>`) plus `from_id`/`to_id` referencing the waypoints it connects. `from`/`to` remain plain display text -- edit them freely, they are what the app shows; they are not used for lookups, so editing them cannot break waypoint/crossing linkage. crossings.json and alerts.json reference waypoints/segments by id, not by name or order, so renaming a waypoint's `name` or renumbering segments never breaks those links.
